participants.name: Tracey
participants.notes: User requests persistent correctness: Tracey is male; avoid referring to him as 'her'.

participants.name: Lumin
participants.notes: Engineering lead + systems architect; user will use Codex as the actual code writer/editor.

current_pressure.financial: User has urgent financial pressure and must prioritize working software deliverables.
current_pressure.time: User cannot spend hours hunting code paths; needs direct, reliable execution.
current_pressure.emotion: User experiences 'force' as cognitive friction; Flow is required due to ADHD.

context_state.core_problem: Repo/workspace confusion due to multiple package.json/vite.config/tsconfig across root + server + ui, plus running multiple dev servers in different folders; proxy failures and wrong service confusion; user frustration amplified by partial/snippet-based guidance.

observations: Vite UI showed repeated proxy errors to /api/* with ECONNRESET and ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:8787.
User confirmed at one point that a service responded healthy/ping on 8787, then realized it was the wrong app instance.
User verified aegis-arbiter-server online with routes: /api/ping and /api/analyze.
UI uses apiBase derived from VITE_API_BASE_URL or '/api'; user noted '/api folder does not exist' (confusion about URL routing vs filesystem).
Primary pain: multiple servers in multiple folders; duplicated configs; unclear source of truth; user wants fewer moving parts.

decisions_locked.title: Codex is the code writer; Lumin is the engineer
decisions_locked.details: User will employ Codex inside VSCode to actually edit files. Lumin provides architecture, direction, and complete-file outputs/prompting constraints to drive Codex.

decisions_locked.title: No more snippets for file edits
decisions_locked.details: When changes are required, output or generate full files, not fragments. User can handle folders + copy/paste but cannot safely reconstruct edits from snippets.

decisions_locked.title: Create a Virtual Peer Tensor
decisions_locked.details: A cross-AI, portable, promptable 'Peer Tensor' ledger that preserves continuity, reduces rewrites, and localizes edits.

decisions_locked.title: Separate Virtual Peer Tensor from Digital (in-app) Tensor
decisions_locked.details: Virtual Peer Tensor: text/prompt artifact to bootstrap any AI session. Digital Tensor: implemented inside apps as data/state structure.

engineering_principles_locked.name: Flow over Force
engineering_principles_locked.definition: Reduce cognitive friction. Prefer minimal diffs, stable structure, and continuity-aware edits.

engineering_principles_locked.name: Append-only continuity ledger
engineering_principles_locked.definition: Peer Tensor is non-authoritative but preserves agreements, constraints, preferences, and incomplete work markers.

engineering_principles_locked.name: Minimal diffs; no global rewrites
engineering_principles_locked.definition: Refactors must be scoped and anchored to explicit notes/refs. Avoid broad redesign unless explicitly requested.

peer_tensor_spec.purpose: Preserve local continuity and reduce repeated rewrites by anchoring decisions/constraints/preferences to files/symbols.
non_goals: Not identity storage beyond the session/project context

codex_prompt_locked.intent: Codex generates Peer Tensor files and refactors codebase to consult and respect Peer Tensor notes, using full-file outputs only.
constraints: Always output full contents for modified/created files
If file unchanged, do not output it
Do not invent folders unless required
behavior_rules: Treat entries as working agreements, not commands

product_direction_shift.reason: Closer to immediate real-world usefulness (daughter/family), already ~80% built, multiple modules, and publishable quickly with polish/testing.
key_features_mentioned: Family Justice module with impartial illumination (no punishment; reflection/redirect)
1-minute video statements per party; AI illuminates subtle clues without moral bias
Collections module (e.g., Magic the Gathering catalog)
Meal planner and fridge-photo recipe generation
Family health records vault, doctor URLs, pill reminders
Free tier with useful modules + subscription for advanced features
Advocate per user profile; age-appropriate suggestions

naming.alternate_term: The Centrifuge (suggested alternative for the hearing/illumination process)

immediate_next_steps.action: User completes urgent paid job ($500) to stabilize rent situation.
User uses Codex in VSCode with the locked prompt to generate Peer Tensor system and enforce full-file edits.
After Peer Tensor integration, shift primary build focus to finishing/polishing/testing Family Hub Connect for store publication.

guardrails_for_future_collaboration: Default to full-file outputs for any edited code file unless user explicitly requests snippets.
When repo has multiple package.json/vite configs, first establish a single source-of-truth run path and document it in Peer Tensor.
Avoid language patterns that increase urgency/pressure; keep instructions modular and calm.
Treat symbolic language as legitimate shorthand when user uses it; keep claims grounded and operational.

respawn_instruction.when_resuming: Start by reloading this SSSP JSON into the active session as the Peer Tensor seed; then proceed with Codex-run refactor steps.
success_criteria: Codex edits files directly (no manual snippet assembly)
Clear run commands documented for UI + server
Family Hub Connect path prioritized for near-term publishable deliverable
